LOOK.text: Three monitors: portfolio performance, yield farming stats, and NFT marketplace analytics.

USE.text: The terminal runs a sophisticated trading bot with AI algorithms.

TALK.text: The AI bot says: 'I've been outperforming humans since 2024.'

PICK.text: There's a smartphone on the desk with crypto trading apps still open. The battery is dead. Classic.

then.text: The drawer is empty. I already took the trading terminal key.

else.text: I open the desk drawer and find a trading terminal key with significant access!
There's a sticky note inside: 'PZ escaped via contract exploit — check server logs'

CLOSE.text: I close the desk drawer. The trading terminal key is safely stored.

PUSH.text: I push the desk. The monitors wobble. The trading bot panics: 'VOLATILITY DETECTED!'

PULL.text: I pull open a side panel. Just cable management. Immaculate cable management.

GIVE.text: The trading desk doesn't accept deposits. Only the smart contracts do.

LOOK.text: Boxes labeled 'Moon Mission', 'Diamond Hands', 'Lambo Dreams'. All successful!

USE.text: I peek inside one. A limited edition AdrianPunk NFT, sealed and catalogued. This collection isn't meant to be disturbed.

TALK.text: The boxes hum with positive vibes: 'WAGMI...'

PICK.text: There's a rare NFT trading card inside one. It's sealed in a display case — look, don't touch.

OPEN.text: Inside: rare memecoins and governance tokens, all neatly organized. Adrian was a collector, not a hoarder. Well... maybe both.

CLOSE.text: I close the box. The valuable NFTs are safely stored.

PUSH.text: I push the boxes. They're heavier than my bags. And my bags are heavy.

PULL.text: I pull a box off the stack. 'Moon Mission' falls and spills confetti. Bullish confetti.

GIVE.text: I can't give away someone else's moon missions. That's not how airdrops work.

LOOK.text: A high-end setup with multiple GPUs. Mining and trading powerhouse.

then.text: The rig recognizes my $ZERO balance! It shows me the secret TraitLab dashboard.

else.text: The computer runs TraitLab and the openPack program. Needs 1000 $ZERO for full access.

TALK.text: The GPUs hum in harmony: 'Hash... hash... hash...'

PICK.text: There's a USB drive plugged in, labeled 'STRATEGIES'. It's encrypted and soldered to the rig. Not going anywhere.

OPEN.text: I open the case. High-end GPUs and cooling systems inside.

CLOSE.text: I close the case. The mining continues uninterrupted.

PUSH.text: I push the power button. The rig reboots. Hash rate: temporarily zero. Like my portfolio in 2022.

PULL.text: I pull out a GPU. It's warm. Like holding a small sun that turns electricity into disappointment.

GIVE.text: I'm not giving away GPUs. Do you know what these cost during the shortage?

LOOK.text: Where successful traders sit and watch portfolios grow. Built-in massage features.

USE.text: I sit in the armchair. Incredibly comfortable. This is what making it feels like.

TALK.text: The chair purrs: 'You deserve this, diamond hands.'

PICK.text: Something glints under the cushion — a luxury watch worth several ETH. It's tangled in the springs. I'd need tools to get it out.

CLOSE.text: The compartments are already closed and secure.

PUSH.text: I push the armchair. It reclines automatically. Luxury is heavy.

PULL.text: I pull the lever underneath. Full recline mode. I could watch charts from here all day.

GIVE.text: This chair is non-transferable. Soulbound furniture.

LOOK.text: A ceiling hatch with a pull-down ladder. It leads to the rooftop. Stars peek through the gap.

TALK.text: The hatch creaks. Wind whistles through the gap.

PICK.text: I can't pick up a ceiling hatch. Gravity disagrees.

CLOSE.text: I close the hatch. The stars disappear.

PUSH.text: I push the hatch upward. It opens easily.

GIVE.text: The hatch doesn't want gifts. It wants to be climbed.

LOOK.text: An articulated desk lamp. The only source of light in this cave of a trading floor. It's seen some late-night liquidations.

USE.text: I angle the lamp toward the desk. The charts look even more terrifying in direct light.

TALK.text: 'Shine a light on these losses, will you?' The lamp obliges. I immediately regret asking.

PICK.text: The lamp is bolted to the desk and still warm.

OPEN.text: I open the lampshade. A dead moth falls out. It died doing what it loved: chasing the light.

CLOSE.text: I fold the lamp down. Darkness returns. The green glow of monitors is somehow more ominous now.

PUSH.text: I push the lamp arm. It springs back. Resilient, like a true HODLer.

PULL.text: I pull the lamp closer. My shadow looms over the trading screens like a bearish engulfing pattern.

GIVE.text: The lamp doesn't want gifts. It just wants someone to change its bulb once in a while.

LOOK.text: An open pizza box on the floor. Two slices remain, petrified. The receipt says '10,000 BTC'. Just kidding. But someone definitely ate here for days.

USE.text: I consider eating the ancient pizza. My survival instincts kick in just in time. This pizza predates the last halving.

TALK.text: 'How long have you been here?' The pizza doesn't answer. It's transcended the concept of freshness.

PICK.text: I pick up a petrified slice. It could be used as a weapon. Or a coaster. I put it back.

OPEN.text: Already open. The pizza box has been in this state longer than most DeFi protocols.

CLOSE.text: I close the box. A small mercy for anyone else who enters this room.

PUSH.text: I push the box under the desk. Out of sight, out of mind. Like unrealized losses.

PULL.text: I pull the box toward me. The smell hits. That's not pizza anymore. That's a biohazard.

GIVE.text: Nobody wants week-old pizza. Not even the rats. And rats eat literally anything.

LOOK.text: A half-empty energy drink can. The label says 'BULL RUN: 500mg Caffeine.' The fuel of every 3 AM trading session.

USE.text: I take a sip. It's flat and warm. Like the market after a hype cycle. My heart rate increases anyway.

TALK.text: 'Give me energy!' The can is silent. It already gave everything it had. Respect.

then.text: I already grabbed the can. One dose of regret is enough.

else.text: I grab the can. Still some liquid sloshing around. Could be useful for... something. Or someone. Or a dying plant.

OPEN.text: Already open. The tab was pulled with the same urgency as a margin call notification.

CLOSE.text: You can't un-open a can. Like you can't un-send a transaction. Irreversible.

PUSH.text: I push the can. It rolls across the floor leaving a sticky trail. Like slippage on a DEX.

PULL.text: I pull the can toward me. The last drops of BULL RUN slosh around sadly.

GIVE.text: Nobody wants a half-empty energy drink. It's the NFT of beverages — unique but valueless.

LOOK.text: A paper tray on the desk. There's a crumpled receipt sitting on top, half-buried under printouts of failed transactions.

USE.text: I shuffle through the papers. Mostly gas fee receipts and 'Transaction Failed' confirmations.

TALK.text: The paper tray rustles in the draft. Even the paperwork is trying to escape this place.

then.text: I already grabbed the receipt. The tray is just sad printouts now.

else.text: I grab the receipt. It's a printout of a massive transaction — coordinates to a clinic in the mountains. Someone was tracking Patient Zero.

OPEN.text: It's an open tray. Nothing to open. Like my positions — always exposed.

CLOSE.text: Paper trays don't close. They just accumulate regret in paper form.

PUSH.text: I push the tray aside. Papers scatter like panicked traders during a flash crash.

PULL.text: I pull the tray closer. The papers whisper tales of failed swaps and missed airdrops.

GIVE.text: The paper tray has enough bad news. It doesn't need more from me.

then.text: Founder mode is already active. The mining rig hums contentedly.

else.text: I insert the Golden ZERO Token into a slot on the mining rig. The screens flash: 'GOLDEN KEY ACCEPTED — FOUNDER MODE UNLOCKED.'
An admin panel appears. Adrian's full ecosystem dashboard.
The admin dashboard lights up. FloorEngine: 847 sweeps. TraitLab: 12,459 traits minted. SweepBot: running 24/7 for 403 days.
Adrian built all of this... and then disappeared. Why?

script.text: I insert a floppy from the box into the trading desk's reader. The three monitors sync instantly.
The screens display: 'BACKUP TRADING STRATEGIES FOUND — FLOPPY ARCHIVE v1.0. Last modified: 3 AM, obviously.'
Strategy #1: 'Buy when mass is fearful.' Strategy #2: 'Sell when mass is greedy.' Strategy #3: 'Ignore strategies 1 and 2. Ape in. YOLO. NFA.'
The AI trading bot interrupts: 'These strategies predate me by two years and outperformed me by 340%. I am humbled. And slightly offended.'
At the bottom of the file: 'NOTE: If you own these floppies, you already understood the best strategy — collect the art, ignore the noise.' Smart.
ALPHA LEAK — Found Adrian's backup trading strategies
I hold a floppy disc near the mining rig's USB port. Wait — floppy discs don't use USB. But the rig detects it anyway.
The GPUs whir louder. The screen displays: 'ADRIANLAB FLOPPY — GENESIS CONFIG DETECTED. Loading original mining parameters from 2024...'
The rig shows Adrian's first-ever mining config. Hash rate: pathetic. Power bill: astronomical. Conviction: immeasurable.
A GPU lights up with a hidden overclocking profile labeled 'FLOPPY_HODLER_BOOST'. The hash rate spikes. The rig hums a thank you.
The screen settles on a message: 'This rig mined the first block that powered AdrianLAB. Your floppies carry its DNA. Digital archaeology at its finest.'
GENESIS MINER — Activated the rig's original config
